rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM H BRADLEY VS. DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Rsma Foreclosure Services, Llc To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Appear For Deposition; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,311; Declaration Of Brian Newman; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT CIT BANK, N.A.(F/K/A ONE WEST BANK N.A.)'s Motion To Compel RSMA Foreclosure Services, LLC To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Appear For Deposition; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,311 Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is unopposed and is granted. Cross-Complainant RSM&A Foreclosure Services, LLC ("RSM&A") shall within 10 days, (1) provide complete, verified responses to CIT's First Set of Special Interrogatories; (2) provide complete, verified responses to CIT's First Set of Requests for Production of Documents, and produce any and all responsive, non-privileged documents; and (3) appear for deposition pursuant to CIT's Notice of Deposition. Sanctions are awarded against RSM&A in the amount of $2,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OLATUNJI O RAHEEM VS. GOODFELLOW TOP GRADE CONSTRUCTION, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF OLATUNJI RAHEEM'S Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Plaintiff Olatunji O. Raheem's motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement is denied without prejudice. In order to determine whether a proposed class action settlement is fair and reasonable, a trial court "should consider relevant factors, such as the strength of plaintiffs' case, the risk, expense, complexity and likely duration of further litigation, the risk of maintaining class action status through trial, the amount offered in settlement, the extent of discovery completed and the stage of the proceedings, the experience and views of counsel, the presence of a governmental participant, and the reaction of the class members to the proposed settlement." (Dunk v. Ford Motor Co. 48 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1801.) "'The most important factor is the strength of the case for plaintiffs on the merits, balanced against the amount offered in settlement.'" (Kullar v. Foot Locker Retail, Inc. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 116, 130.) While a presumption of fairness exists if certain factors are present, "an informed evaluation cannot be made without an understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation." (Id. at 120.) Here, as in Kullar, "nothing was presented to the court-no discovery, no declarations, no time records, no payroll data, nothing [citations]-to allow the court to evaluate the claim." (Munoz v. BCI Coca-Cola Bottling Co. of Los Angeles 186 Cal.App.4th 399, 409.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient information to enable the court to evaluate the reasonableness of the proposed settlement, such as the damages that each class member sustained, the total damages the class could expect to recover if it prevails on its claim, or any explanation of the factors that were considering in discounting the potential recovery for purposes of settlement. (See Kullar, 168 Cal.App.4th at 128-131.) "While the court need not determine the ultimate merit of a claim, it is obliged to determine, at a minimum, whether a legitimate controversy exists on a legal point, so that it has some basis for assessing whether the parties' evaluation of the case is within the 'ballpark' of reasonableness." (Clark v. American Residential Services LLC (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 785, 790.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY E. HELD PHD VS. FORNEY INDUSTRIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And (Proposed) Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And (Proposed) Consent Judgment Plaintiff Anthony E. Held, Ph.D., P.E.'s motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning and/or injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint Defendant Dignity Community Care dba Saint Francis Memorial Hospital's demurrer to Plaintiff Haiqing Qiu's complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's sole cause of action for medical malpractice is governed by the one-year limitations provision of Code of Civil Procedure section 340.5. The complaint and Plaintiff's opposition indicate that the alleged malpractice occurred on October 18, 2017, when Plaintiff alleges he witnessed medical staff refusing to treat his mother and prematurely declaring her deceased. The complaint was not filed until January 16, 2019, more than one year after Plaintiff discovered the alleged malpractice, and therefore appears on its face to be barred by the statute of limitations. However, Plaintiff asserts in his opposition (at pp. 18, 20-21) that on August 15, 2018, he filed a claim and demanded compensation from Defendant for his mother's death and Defendant's alleged malpractice. That claim may qualify as a 90-day notice of intent to sue within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure § 364. "No particular form of notice is required, but it shall notify the defendant of the legal basis of the claim and the type of loss sustained, including with specificity the nature of the injuries suffered." (Code Civ. Proc. § 364(b); see Kumari v. The Hospital Committee for the Livermore-Pleasanton Areas (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 306, 312-315 [plaintiff's letter to hospital describing her injury and the basis for her medical negligence claim constituted a notice of intent to sue within the meaning of Section 364].) Service of a notice of intent to sue during the last 90 days of the one-year period tolls the statute for 90 days. (Id. § 364(d); Woods v. Young (1991) 53 Cal.3d 315, 325.) Suit therefore is timely if filed within 1 year and 90 days after discovery of the injury. (Woods, 53 Cal.3d at 325.) Here, Plaintiff filed this suit on January 16, 2019-exactly one year and 90 days after the alleged negligence. Plaintiff therefore has 20 days leave to file an amended complaint including the allegations regarding the claim made to Defendant on August 15, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint Defendant Dignity Community Care dba Saint Francis Memorial Hospital's motion to strike the claim for attorney's fees in Plaintiff's complaint is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff has not alleged any statutory or contractual basis for an award of attorney's fees. (Reynolds Metals Co. v. Alperson (1975) 25 Cal.3d 124, 127 ["Unless authorized by either statute or agreement, attorney's fees ordinarily are not recoverable as costs."].) The amended complaint should not include a request for attorney's fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICES, INC. VS. B&H BUILDERS ASSOCIATES INC. A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike The Answer Of Deft B&H Builders Associates, Inc.; Memo Of P&A; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Andre J. Lelievre
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 10, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Strike The Answer Of Deft B&H Builders Associates, Inc. Plaintiff Northern California Collection Services, Inc.'s motion to strike answer is granted. Judicially noticeable documents show that defendant improperly filed an answer in this case on March 5, 2019, even though it has been a suspended corporation since December 1, 2016. A suspended corporation lacks the capacity to defend a lawsuit. (See Rev. & Tax. Code § 23301; Bourhis v. Lord (2013) 56 Cal.4th 320, 324.) The court normally would continue this matter in order to give defendant an opportunity to obtain a certificate of revivor from the Franchise Tax Board. However, because Plaintiff's counsel placed defendant on notice of its incapacity in March 2019 and Defendant has failed to oppose this motion, the court will strike the answer without leave to amend pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc., § 435 and enter default against defendant B&H Builders Associates, Inc. Plaintiff has leave to seek a default judgment against defendant B&H Builders Associates, Inc. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
